body:
Fixes #1831

## Summary
- Added `UnlockNotificationManager`, a global CanvasLayer toast that listens to `UnlockManager` availability signals during play.
- Shows queued top-screen unlock-availability notifications with the Armory icon, localized text, a gold frame, slide-in/slide-out motion, and the existing gold shine shader.
- Updated the toast text after review so queued unlocks include the section and display name, for example `Открыт предмет Бронированная кожа !`.
- Keeps the toast lifecycle explicit (`entering`, `visible`, `exiting`, `idle`) and preserves a single scheduled slide-out segment per toast.
- Seeds already-available locked items on startup so old save progress does not replay stale notifications; permanent unlock still happens through the Armory hold action.
- Fixed the feedback case where startup-available locked items were treated as already announced forever. Startup suppression is now separate from actual announcement history, so a later live stat signal can still show Armored Skin, Laser Sight, and similar notifications once.
- Fixed the text visibility feedback by keeping the toast content parent fully opaque and fading only the background/shine layers, so the gold label remains visible after the slide-in animation.
- Fixed the latest textless-toast feedback by changing the toast host from `PanelContainer` to plain `Control` and giving render layers explicit z-indexes: background `0`, shine `1`, content `10`.
- Hardened `SceneLoader` fallback handling after the latest exported exe gray-screen report: fallback scene-change errors are now logged and the loading overlay is only hidden after a successful scene change.
- Added exported-build `RoguelikeLevel` startup milestones to `FileLogger` so future logs show whether `_ready()`, room construction, and player spawn were reached.
- Added issue case-study data, online research notes, owner runtime log analysis, CI snapshots, local validation notes, and a visual preview.

## Visual Preview
![Unlock notification preview](https://github.com/konard/Jhon-Crow-godot-topdown-MVP/blob/issue-1831-8dd128f0f3cf/docs/case-studies/issue-1831/unlock-notification-preview.png?raw=true)

## Feedback Follow-up
- Archived owner logs:
  - `docs/case-studies/issue-1831/attachments/game_log_20260417_003202.txt`
  - `docs/case-studies/issue-1831/attachments/game_log_20260417_023049.txt`
  - `docs/case-studies/issue-1831/attachments/game_log_20260417_205951.txt`
  - `docs/case-studies/issue-1831/attachments/game_log_20260417_221135.txt`
- First feedback log showed repeated Fine Motor Skills availability signals at local log times 00:37:21, 00:37:44, and 00:37:46; notification dedupe remains key-based while animation state is explicit.
- Second feedback log showed persisted stats already above thresholds at startup (`kills_without_laser_sight: 448`, `shots_fired_special_weapons: 654`, `total_deaths: 125`) and later runtime crossings at 100 deaths and 400 kills. Root cause: startup seeding used the same table as actual shown notifications, suppressing live toasts. Fixed by separating startup-suppressed keys from announced keys.
- Text visibility feedback on 2026-04-17 said the label was visible only for a few milliseconds during entry and then disappeared. Root cause: the toast container alpha fade could also fade/mask child content after animation. Fix: the `PanelContainer` and label stayed at alpha 1.0 while only `ToastBackground` and `GoldShineOverlay` animated alpha.
- Latest textless-toast feedback on 2026-04-17 showed Armored Skin availability at local log time 22:18:47, proving unlock detection and signal connection worked. Remaining root cause: the decorative full-rect layers could cover content in exported rendering. Fix: use a plain `Control` host with explicit z-indexes so content renders above shine/background.
- Latest gray-screen feedback on 2026-04-17 showed startup navigation from `LabyrinthLevel` to saved `RoguelikeLevel`, `THREAD_LOAD_INVALID_RESOURCE` fallback, and then no exported-build Roguelike room/player milestones. The scene loader now keeps the overlay on scene-change failure and logs fallback errors; Roguelike startup now logs `_ready`, room construction, and player spawn milestones for the next exported exe validation.

## CI Follow-up
- Fixed the Run GUT Tests timeout by adding the missing `FileLogger.warn()` compatibility alias used by `EnemyTeleportComponent`.
- Stabilized `test_enemy_teleport_component` by invoking `_ready()` directly for parent-resolution cases instead of awaiting frames in the autoload-heavy headless test runner.
- Aligned the unlock notification fallback string with the reviewed/tested text: `Открыт ... !`.
- Merged current `upstream/main` into the branch to clear the PR dirty merge state while preserving both `UnlockNotificationManager` and the new `LevelLocalization` autoload.
- Added SceneLoader regression coverage for failed scene changes keeping the loading overlay visible and successful scene changes hiding it.

## Tests
- `git diff --check` - passed
- `git diff --cached --check` - passed before commit `884bd2cf`
- `dotnet build` - passed with existing warnings, 0 errors
- Godot/GUT targeted rerun was not available in this workspace because no `godot`/`godot4`/`Godot_v4.3-stable_mono_linux.x86_64` binary was present; regression tests are committed in `tests/unit/test_unlock_notification_manager.gd` and `tests/unit/test_scene_loader.gd`.
- Previous targeted CI/local checks also passed:
  - `test_unlock_notification_manager` - passed 8/8 tests, 29 assertions before the latest text-layer follow-up
  - `test_enemy_teleport_component` - passed 19/19
- New CI for `884bd2cf` starts after this push.


- Latest textless-toast follow-up after owner feedback on 2026-04-17: the toast content layer now receives explicit runtime sizes for the margin container and content row, preventing exported builds from laying out the label at zero/incorrect dimensions while the background still renders. Added regression assertions for non-zero content geometry.
title: Add unlock notification toast
